title: Consolida pusilla
intro: Consolida pusilla, also known as small larkspur or small delphinium, is a flowering plant species in the borage family (Boraginaceae) native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, fever, and digestive issues, due to its expectorant, antiseptic, and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Consolida pusilla include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Consolida pusilla include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural expectorant.
taxonomy: Consolida pusilla is classified within the family Papaveraceae. This family is commonly known as the poppy family. Consolida pusilla is further classified within the genus Consolida, which consists of a group of small, annual flowering plants. The genus Consolida is often divided from the genus Papaver due to distinct differences in their seed pods. Consolida pusilla is also known as the dwarf larkspur, indicating its small stature and the presence of spurs on its flowers.
